parameters.name: pH
parameters.description: pH measures how acidic or basic the water is.
parameters.visual_test: Match the test strip color to the pH chart.

parameters.name: Clarity
parameters.description: Clarity shows how much sediment, algae, or suspended material is in the sample.
parameters.visual_test: Look through the sample jar at a marked card.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.safe_range: cooler water generally holds more oxygen
parameters.description: Water temperature affects oxygen levels and which organisms can live there.
parameters.visual_test: Compare the thermometer reading against the habitat chart.

parameters.name: Dissolved Oxygen
parameters.safe_range: higher is usually better for fish and insects
parameters.description: Dissolved oxygen is the oxygen available for aquatic animals to breathe.
parameters.visual_test: Use indicator drops and compare the color to the oxygen scale.

parameters.name: Nitrates
parameters.description: High nitrate levels can feed algae blooms, often after fertilizer runoff.
parameters.visual_test: Use a nitrate strip and compare it to the nutrient chart.

parameters.name: Macroinvertebrate Indicators
parameters.description: Small aquatic animals can indicate long-term water quality.
parameters.visual_test: Sort tray organisms into sensitive, tolerant, and very tolerant groups.

indicator_species.name: Mayfly Nymph
indicator_species.meaning: Usually prefers healthier water with good oxygen.

indicator_species.name: Dragonfly Nymph
indicator_species.meaning: Can live in a range of habitats but still needs a functioning food web.

indicator_species.name: Midge Larva
indicator_species.meaning: Can tolerate poorer water, especially when oxygen is lower.
